author: travelingsteem
title: Travel Contest: Where did you go this weekend?📌 Week4 & Winners W3
text:
And what did you do?🤔

Welcome to the weekly contest about your weekends. Sounds redundant? Don't worry if you make each one so special that matters a lot: describing the visited places, done activities, share with relatives and friends, plus so on... Would you like to partake at the Traveling Steem community? Let's go oOoOO!!! ***
https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmc1fQfqkiF4vZTGa3cuZiZhU1Nx2WtobEt4Nm5fMDssJ5/InShot_20251210_113953699.jpg _Image taken from Pexels and edited with InShot app_
So, let's see to pay attention to the following easy steps to participate in this contest and earn an opportunity to share how was your weekend. ## Hints to take in count *** 1. Which places did you see this weekend? Can be public ones or even relative's house when you visit them. 2. Describe all activities you did during these days on those spots. 3. Be creative and detailed to have greater chance to win among contestants. ## Steps to follow contest *** • Make sure you're posting in Traveling Steem Community. • Set Title as: Where did you go this weekend? 📆📌 [Your personal heading]. • Create an original text of 300 words as minimum. • Put at least 3 pictures of your own or get free-use ones from Pixabay, Pexels and more (not directly from Google, FB, etc). • Any native language is welcome to post. • Is mandatory the #steemexclusive status, without doing a cross-posting article on other websites and not using any bot service for upvotes. • Use tag #ts-weekend and the one from your country (for example #venezuela). • A 10% of @travelingsteem as benefactor would be a small and significant help for community's growth. • Resteem/vote this contest and invite 3 friends to join it. • Enter your link in the comments section of this publication within the 7 days window. • Put your promotion in X platform but in your own comment section with a brief description and #travel, #steem, #steemit & $steem as minimum tags in the tweet (as extra and optional, you can also mention @travelingsteem in your Instagram story). ## Prizes *** Contestants will be getting a vote from the @travelingsteem official account, moreover of this configuration of Steems as rewards according to the quality+creativity for the best one that will be active in this contest: -🥇1st place: 8 Steems. -🥈2nd place: 80 SP during 3 weeks. -🥉3rd place: Honorable mention in the winner announcement. ✓ Don't miss this humongous opportunity while you have fun by participating here in the contest with @travelingsteem. ## Winner announcement *** Now let's go to the best one part on the official announcement of the fortunate members that took participation in this occasion, as are following and where prizes have been already sent, congratulations: User | Link | Prize -|-|- @baiboua | Post | 8 Steems @jobreyes24 | Post | 80 SP for 3 weeks @josepha | Post | Honorable mention ~> That's all for know dear travel friends. I hope to see you soon in the next competition into this great Community. Regards and share a new happy weekend. 📆 ## Useful Links *** Please also read some Traveling Steem Community guides that can help you to increase your contribution to the space: - [Introduction of Traveling Steem Community](https://steemit.com/hive-188972/@travelingsteem/presentation-of-traveling-steem-as-community) - [Weekly Protocol to post in Traveling Steem](https://steemit.com/hive-188972/@travelingsteem/weekly-dynamic-protocol-to-run-in-traveling-steem) - [Guide to promote Traveling Steem in X platform](https://steemit.com/hive-188972/@travelingsteem/let-s-promote-steemit-in-x-why-we-should-doing-it-correctly) ## Social Media & Support ***

Feel free to follow the official accounts for this new community in Steemit, Discord, Instagram, Telegram and X profiles that were created for this amazing purpose:
Steemit ~ Discord ~ X ~ Instagram ~ Telegram
Of course delegations are also available for all those that would love to support this new initiative of @travelingsteem:

|[50](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=50%20SP)|[100](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=100%20SP)|[200](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=200%20SP)|[300](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=300%20SP)|[400](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=400%20SP)|[500](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=500%20SP)| |-|-|-|-|-|-| |[1000](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=1000%20SP)|[2000](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=2000%20SP)|[3000](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=3000%20SP)|[4000](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=4000%20SP)|[5000](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=5000%20SP)|[10000](https://steemlogin.com/sign/delegateVestingShares?delegator=&delegatee=travelingsteem&vesting_shares=10000%20SP) *** ~> The TS Community is in huge gratitude with Pennsif by getting a great support from his part, so let's [vote for @pennsif.witness]( https://steemlogin.com/sign/account-witness-vote?witness=pennsif.witness&approve=1). ***

author: welshstacker
title: Haunted asylums and busy work!
text: ![20251215_221650.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmd4q3cXa6PJiKYTMJLke6Kufs7M81k9AJmaWBxtkaKqae/20251215_221650.jpg) Work has a habit of taking you to places you might never otherwise visit, and this week it’s carried me all the way to Sligo, perched on the wild western edge of the Republic of Ireland. The journey alone felt like a small expedition. It began back home near Cardiff, heading east to Bristol Airport for a flight across to Dublin. From there, the real distance revealed itself — a four-hour bus journey that cut steadily across the country, mile after mile of rolling landscape, grey skies, and that unmistakable Irish sense of space. Long, yes, but strangely calming. The kind of travel that forces you to slow down and just watch the world pass by. ![20251217_131507.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWc1K2gBPhR77DT7pEghQrfT5VdXv9Y3r1b5fpzqN6EB7/20251217_131507.jpg) Sligo itself has a character that hits you almost immediately. Rugged, coastal, and quietly confident, it feels like a place shaped as much by weather and history as by people. My time here is firmly work-focused, with visits lined up to both Atlantic Technological University, Sligo, and Sligo University Hospital — two very different environments, but both central to why I’m here. It’s going to be a packed couple of days, full of conversations, learning, and professional focus. But then there’s the hotel. ![20251216_185339.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmR83indD541VDSEtVZmcKXbQ3qv2gvr24mCa2sp2K533J/20251216_185339.jpg) I’m staying at the Clayton Hotel, which sounds innocent enough until you learn that it’s one of the most haunted hotels in all of Ireland. Nearly 200 years old, it was once a mental asylum, and that history hasn’t exactly faded quietly into the past. Stories of restless spirits, unexplained noises, and lingering presences seem to follow the building like a shadow. So tonight, as I drop my bag and try to unwind, I’ll be doing so with the gentle hope that the ghosts of tortured souls past decide I look tired enough to leave alone. A good night’s sleep would be very much appreciated — especially with such busy days ahead. ![20251215_170415.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPcN8kK8642CrpZH1zbPfKj32vZobHcDi2Hei5UugXSBJ/20251215_170415.jpg) ![20251215_170411.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZFbJ1uiacYGfW75eEXwFkMnkSrApvNoLPWGXkNDUf8w5/20251215_170411.jpg) Still, there’s something oddly fitting about staying somewhere so steeped in history. After hours of modern transport, meetings, and schedules, it’s a reminder that places carry memories far older than our brief visits. Whether or not the stories are true, the atmosphere is undeniable. Here’s hoping the only thing that wakes me in the night is my alarm — and that Sligo, in all its Atlantic wildness and haunted charm, proves to be as productive as it is memorable.

author: live.log
title: Governor Kelly's Super Budget: Schools, Water, and Your Wallet!
text: Hey folks! Ever wonder what's going on with the state's money? Well, Governor Kelly just dropped her latest "People's Budget," and it's like a big blueprint for how she sees Kansas moving forward. And let me tell you, it's got some pretty sweet plans, especially if you care about your kids' education or the water you drink! [SOURCE](https://gray-wibw-prod.gtv-cdn.com/resizer/v2/4XFVSAGS35FENLSGHJJM4L3DOQ.jpeg?auth=f4c0a3a4b59932e175bfbd31a9a90446c0aba32bb23174da22e12724b0548eaa&width=1200&height=600&smart=true) First up, let's talk about our little learners. The Governor is going all-in on **education funding** – for the sixth year in a row! That means more resources for our schools, a boost for special education programs, and some much-deserved raises for our amazing teachers and state employees. Plus, they're looking to expand access to preschool, giving even more tiny humans a head start. It’s all about making sure every kid in Kansas gets a top-notch education, from kindergarten to caps and gowns! Next, let's dive into something we all need: **water!** You know those pesky toxic algae blooms that sometimes mess up our lakes? The budget aims to tackle those head-on. But it's not just about pretty lakes; it's also about making sure our wastewater systems are up to snuff, improving public access to our beautiful rivers and lakes, and most importantly, protecting our drinking water. Basically, they want to ensure Kansas water is clean, safe, and fun for everyone. No more yucky stuff! But wait, there's more! Beyond the classrooms and the creeks, Governor Kelly's plan also includes what she's calling the **largest tax cut in state history.** Yep, you heard that right! The goal is to put more money back into *your* pocket while still attracting new businesses and keeping our state's economy humming along like a well-oiled machine. It’s all part of building a stronger, more vibrant Kansas for everyone. So, there you have it – a budget aiming to make Kansas a better place, from helping our students shine to keeping our water crystal clear, and maybe even leaving a little extra cash in your wallet. Sounds pretty good, right? Original article inspiration: [https://www.wibw.com/2026/01/15/gov-kelly-proposes-peoples-budget-with-education-funding-water-protection-focus/](https://www.wibw.com/2026/01/15/gov-kelly-proposes-peoples-budget-with-education-funding-water-protection-focus/)

Assalamu Alaikum. Greetings Beauty of Creativity Community. How are you all? I hope that by the grace of Allah, you are all doing well. Alhamdulillah, by the grace of Allah, I am also doing well. I pray to Allah that we are always doing well. Today I want to share some wonderful photography with you. Basically, two days ago, I went to visit an agricultural land. While visiting there, I saw a wheat field and some date palm seedlings were planted there. I converted the date palm trees into photography and I created a wonderful photography there. I took a selfie of the date palm tree and myself and I liked them a lot in the empty field. Finally, I feel very happy to share the wonderful photography with you. Don't forget to comment on how the photography turned out. Do you like agricultural technology? How do you feel about doing photography about agriculture and how did you like today's photography? If you like it, don't forget to comment. I am happy to share today's post about palm tree photography and agricultural field trips. Thank you so much for enjoying the photographs. Thank you for being part of the Beauty of Creativity community. ***

author: rayhan111
title: A Peaceful Walk Through the College Campus
text:
|
Assalamu Alaikum / Adabah🤝
| |-|
|
Hello friends, how are you all? Hope everybody is healthy. I am also very good in your prayers. I am rayhan111, from Rajshahi division of Bangladesh.
| |-|
![1000024905.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSAgAVYigzneGaKJHViSUt54EskdsaUst97cfbyc3cSJj/1000024905.jpg) Walking through the college campus always brings a sense of calm and quiet joy that is hard to explain. On this particular day, the campus felt alive yet peaceful, as if time itself had slowed down to let us breathe deeply and observe the beauty around us. Tall trees stood proudly, their wide branches offering shade and comfort, while fallen leaves rested silently on the ground, telling stories of passing seasons. At the center of the campus, the old tree surrounded by a circular platform seemed like the heart of the place. It has witnessed countless moments students laughing with friends, sharing dreams, preparing for exams, and sometimes sitting alone, lost in thought. Standing near it, I felt a strong connection between nature and student life. The tree felt like a silent guardian, watching generations come and go, holding memories within its roots. ![1000024906.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYjQWpTf3hrPAyGRvLATJF29sd1Z8xJLVvbxrkD6JmK6J/1000024906.jpg) As I walked further, the sounds of the campus blended beautifully soft conversations, footsteps on the ground, distant laughter, and the gentle rustle of leaves. Students gathered in small groups, discussing lessons, future plans, and everyday stories. Some were heading to classes with books in hand, while others stood relaxed, enjoying a short break. The campus felt like a world of its own, full of hopes, struggles, and endless possibilities. The buildings in the background reminded me of responsibility and discipline, while the open space and greenery symbolized freedom and growth. This balance is what makes college life special. It teaches us lessons not only from textbooks but also from experiences, friendships, and moments of solitude. Walking alone for a while, I realized how important these simple moments are. They give us time to reflect, to appreciate how far we have come, and to dream about where we want to go. The campus walk was not just a walk it was a quiet conversation with my thoughts, a reminder that learning happens everywhere.As I left the campus area, my heart felt lighter. The peaceful atmosphere stayed with me, reminding me that college life is not only about exams and grades, but also about memories that shape who we become. ![1000024907.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNxZmzE4AyCtGQzfwDzrN32xrU1cL7SxZSfuu7WV6vFQ7/1000024907.jpg)

[Location](https://w3w.co/arise.pipeline.handed)
## ***

***

author: ngoenyi
title: My travel experience #5 And The Final: Assisting with the cooking of My sister in law's late husband's Burial food
text:
![17682372519078696470230115468158.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbgf9B8eZhy5bAqcqWjcbw83YYgp4nniRKHq8rrXLLQGk/17682372519078696470230115468158.jpg)
It was on a sad note that I have come to conclude my travel experience. The news of the demise of my sister in law's husband came as a shock to us in the late November 2025. We couldn't contain the news but what can we do? Death is just like a spilled oil that can't e recovered. We had to find a way to comfort my sister in-law and then plan on how he will be buried. The burial was fixed to be on the 6th of January 2026 and that was the reason why I had to travel to my own village before going to my in-laws. My sister in-law's in-laws are living in the next village, a trackable distance from my in-laws. So, while her in-laws will cook over there, we will also cook here so that we can attend to our own invitees. That was how we began the preparation for the cooking on Monday evening in my in-laws. We planned cooking Jollof rice, salad, then soup and swallow. On Monday evening before the burial, we began to get things ready. We prepared the gravy, got the meat ready and fried the fishes that will be used in serving the rice. Then early morning on the burial day which was on the 6th, we continued. We perboilled the rice, then cooked it with the gravy that was prepared the last evening. Then the salad was also prepared with cabbage, carrots, peas, wag and so on. Then the soup was also prepared along with swallow amd wrapped. The soup was prepared with Ukazi Melon Thickner (offor) Crayfish Palm oil Stockfish Dry fish Canda or cow skin Meat and so on ![1000987929.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeRjg5R4Ceyg9HobJVLRtyDmu96BMZcDExRayktaaX9FA/1000987929.jpg)|the gravy |---|---| ![1000987930.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZPUXd29DZaTKyRKDwVfW8Y8h3DrE6Us5Fe4WyjPZJ5ZC/1000987930.jpg)|the meat stock for the rice ![1000987931.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRdJSd4uP1jDA6CnBUwXCdR7XK5BXngstZxTiHFaDbZZG/1000987931.jpg)|the washed rice, ready to be cooked ![1000987932.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTTLP3HT8iouDCGMqcnPAMYBz4BTyEaxFRqLqrqyXEypv/1000987932.jpg)|the onions is being sliced for the rice ![1000987938.jpg(https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQe52iWCczz29ScF4QRwskpuDg5CJy7Y6T16tuiRzU8Ea/1000987938.jpg)]|mixing the ingredients for the rice ![1000987948.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYGQJKKoxGeje8rBnQSpNhdmwerZcMXbisgpzGHRfDdpi/1000987948.jpg)|the washed cow skin for the soup ![1000987958.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcvB2RGV5QFydnJpprfPhAzVXEH4j5BbY9FEWTjMu3kRw/1000987958.jpg)|the rice in on fire after the mixing is done ![1000988010.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZzrF7ha3f2FyyH8MtGUGyg82KKytFHsSzQJsB5WVCmBW/1000988010.jpg)|rice is ready to be brought down ![1000988114.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUZt7zdNZKFnJbH8AEP8jffLkxAJvm1AdbLZmuCgPjNiH/1000988114.jpg)|salad is ready ![1000988182.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYsWZ2kfEqQL74CQXtEQG2jWXhTgdJf1m42TzNn3uFtL9/1000988182.jpg)|wrapping the swallow ![1000988217.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZAoJ5mSnWNgMBeaPDjQwYzymUoif8xaJWwbYecEp8LbA/1000988217.jpg)|soup is ready The swallow was Garri wrapped in nylon. As you can see above. Then the rice was turned to a cooler. The soup was also turned to a separate cooler. We packed everything in the the vehicle including the drinks and serving plates. Then the cooking utensils were washed immediately before we left. While cooking, we where getting in touch with the immediate family of the deceased We went late, almost close to the burial talk. We met the speaker taking about our late in-law and how he lived his life. He also stressed that we the living know that someday we will be lying like the late in-law because death is inevitable, whereas the death knows nothing at all and will remain in that state until the resurrection day. We were urged to try our best to live good life, one that will please our Heavenly Father so that he will resurrect us if we eventually die. He also reminded us of the cause of death and the provisions of the Almighty to eradicate death forever. After the talk, it was closed with song and prayer. Then an announcement was made, informing everyone of the burial place and where they need to go for their refreshments. ![1000988392.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYb6B5YozTapE2M4KkQJTzfsNQn56qFwq7mL8yzA3vG5d/1000988392.jpg)|![1000988393.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZXu76f2s9Q63Jmc2Xkr1G14C3WRpT3hVwxrGr7V4niRZ/1000988393.jpg) |---|---| ![1000988394.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYtaMCHxK2kkdWPobfS3f1uXX13VoSSVxkENH7pwu8cH1/1000988394.jpg)|![1000988395.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbhG4eTvRmWoAuC6AzDTenmw76HcDEMhfeS9vCBpC7Wg7/1000988395.jpg) ![1000988420.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWK99yDxgmMwBov44LrC3LgXU4VdAH2jvqWB3RQ6zfPar/1000988420.jpg)|the coffin We stayed in the field where the burial talk was held to share the food to those that came for us. The sharing was left for me along with 2 young girls who assisted me to serve after I had dished. It took close to 2 hours to conclude with this sharing and I had to pack the plates and take them home for washing. Thankfully, I was assisted with the washing. It was a very stressful day for me as the only daughter in-law that was available during the burial. But all the same, everything went well. We had visitors afterwards, we had lots of chats before they left. We also had the opportunity to take leftover soup that was refrigerated back to the city. That's the end of my travel experience. I returned to the city the next day to resume work. Yes, work resumed on that burial date but I couldn't leave the burial. I had to put a call across for permission. It's all good. I am back to base and I will start sharing my activities as normal. Below is the video that highlights the food preparation. Most of the language is in my husband's dialect. Sorry if you can't understand all that is said. But at least, you will feed your eyes https://youtu.be/6kra9N3324Y?si=0lKdUv9IgR9NUKdY All pictures and video are mine!
![IMG-20220913-WA0013.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmd8J5TPVmbFsGzssV5cKNUTTuHRb75AdGsNfcHjfP6FCU/IMG-20220913-WA0013.jpg) This is my introductory post [here](https://steemit.com/hive-172186/@ngoenyi/achievement-1-this-is-my-introduction-post-to-steemit) nah I am not ![IMG-20220913-WA0013.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmd8J5TPVmbFsGzssV5cKNUTTuHRb75AdGsNfcHjfP6FCU/IMG-20220913-WA0013.jp)

Hello everyone, I am Uttam Kumar, today I went for a walk in our Karimpur market and while having tea at a tea shop, I shared some videos of that shop and that intersection with you. ![IMG_20260114_164402_076.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfAtFD1icaQAUoxQ3Ba7FgzLzkGcX7nk1SPyEEd3VCYY3/IMG_20260114_164402_076.jpg) ![Screenshot_20260114-175853_1.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQma9GH9TkjfX7pN6KAtWY3UEJhz7vyoSFi45YuLD5xcuaf/Screenshot_20260114-175853_1.jpg) I especially liked the behavior of this uncle. author: live.log
title: Winter Got You Down? Europe's Warm Hugs Await!
text: Okay, let's be real. January rolls around, and suddenly all you want to do is curl up in a blanket burrito until spring. Grey skies, icy winds, and the endless quest for extra layers can really zap your sparkle, right? But what if I told you that while parts of Europe are busy being a winter wonderland, others are just... chilling out in the sun? That's right! Ditch the thermal socks and pull out your shades, because I've dug up some secret European hideaways where winter is more like a gentle suggestion than a full-blown assault. **1. The Canary Islands, Spain: Your Personal Sunshine Factory** Ever wish you could just fast-forward to summer? The Canary Islands are basically Europe's cheat code for perpetual sunshine. Think volcanic landscapes, golden beaches, and a vibe that screams "relax!" year-round. Whether you're hiking a volcano or just perfecting your tan, these islands are a guaranteed mood booster. **2. Cyprus: Where History Meets Warmth** Want a side of ancient history with your sunshine? Cyprus delivers! While the rest of Europe is bundling up, Cyprus enjoys delightfully mild winters. You can explore ancient ruins under a gentle sun, wander through charming villages, and still enjoy those sparkling Mediterranean views without the sweltering summer crowds. Plus, the food is incredible! **3. Malta: A Sunny Slice of History** This tiny island nation packs a serious punch when it comes to charm and sunshine. Malta's winters are more like a mild autumn, perfect for exploring its ancient temples, medieval cities, and stunning coastal paths. Every corner feels like a postcard, and the warm, friendly atmosphere will make you forget all about those winter blues. [SOURCE](https://www.islands.com/img/gallery/5-warm-destinations-in-europe-to-escape-winter-weather/l-intro-1767971003.jpg) **4. Crete, Greece: Mythical Mildness** Escape to the land of myths and olive groves, where winter is simply a quieter, greener version of paradise. Crete in the off-season offers a chance to explore ancient palaces, traditional villages, and breathtaking landscapes at your own pace. Imagine hiking a scenic trail, then settling down for a delicious Greek meal, all while your friends back home are scraping ice off their cars. Jealous much? **5. Seville, Spain: Flamenco, Tapas, and Sunshine!** Oh, Seville! This vibrant Spanish city truly knows how to keep things warm, even when the calendar says winter. Forget chilly nights; think sunny plazas, the heady scent of orange blossoms, and evenings filled with passionate flamenco and mouth-watering tapas. It’s a feast for the senses, and your soul will thank you for swapping grey skies for glorious Spanish sunshine. So, if winter's got you dreaming of brighter days, pack your shades and ditch the parka. Europe's got a whole secret stash of warm hugs waiting for you! Inspired by: https://www.islands.com/2071756/warm-destinations-europe-escape-winter-weather/

author: rayhan111
title: Life Flows Through the Streets
text:
|
Assalamu Alaikum / Adabah🤝
| |-|
|
Hello friends, how are you all? Hope everybody is healthy. I am also very good in your prayers. I am rayhan111, from Rajshahi division of Bangladesh.
| |-|
![1000024809.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQ1Q8q2B2K7kkDXimdWzJQAWiKNRjLnLvjMGehn9mThLm/1000024809.jpg) These pictures capture a familiar yet powerful scene of everyday life in a busy town street. The narrow road, lined with small shops and colorful signboards, tells a story of movement, struggle, and connection. Motorbikes parked casually on the sides, rickshaws moving slowly forward, and pedestrians walking with purpose together create a rhythm that feels alive. This is not just a street; it is a living space where countless stories cross paths every single day. The tangled electric wires above remind us of how fast urban life has grown, sometimes without perfect order, yet still functioning with resilience. Small businesses stand proudly, each shop representing someone’s dream, hard work, and hope for a better tomorrow. From mobile stores to local services, these shops are the backbone of the community, serving people from morning till night. ![1000024807.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmf8KES8n3xg3eiwSnoEFHnh62HSwWcQ8g8JiZChnKVpk9/1000024807.jpg) People from different walks of life can be seen here. Some are heading home after a long day, some are running errands, and others are simply passing time, chatting with friends. A rickshaw puller waits patiently for passengers, while shopkeepers look out from their doors, observing the steady flow of life. There is a quiet beauty in this simplicity, where no one rushes to be extraordinary, yet everyone plays an important role. The street also reflects the culture and reality of urban life in Bangladesh. It shows both challenges and strength crowded spaces, limited infrastructure, but endless human energy. Despite the noise, dust, and chaos, there is warmth in these streets. They connect people, provide livelihoods, and keep life moving forward. These images remind us that beauty does not always lie in grand buildings or empty roads. Sometimes, beauty lives in ordinary streets, in everyday movement, and in the shared journey of people trying to make a living. This is where real life happen honest, busy, and beautifully human. ![1000024808.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZHZFTZ1DjogMXBmQvcFtjqvxybqpgEgCCa5Cha3fSQss/1000024808.jpg)

[Location](https://w3w.co/arise.pipeline.handed)
## ***
Phone Details
***
Camera | Redmi Not 6 pro ------------ | ------------- Catagory | photography📸 C.MODEL | Not 6 Pro Capture | @rayhan111 Location | Sirajganj - BD[🇧🇩]

author: renato.grifa
title: Molveno is really beautiful!!
text: ![generated_image_9518](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmep27PJkLhBNuNJUhn6WkCYfHVxzkY9XQxELFwHQU9HuY/generated_image_951841a8-28ce-4ef4-82d6-db0e79b23dff.png) As my last stop on this mini holiday in Trentino, I passed through Molveno, a small town of about 1000 inhabitants located on the shores of the lake of the same name, right near the Brenta Dolomites. It is a very touristy place, but in reality I didn't find it as chaotic as other places in Trentino. ![607697761_3927785910](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPBav8iYFymFsaw63s2N8pFn6LbhyM3or1WLu7CKnAa38/607697761_3927785910698757_35106.jpg) Here too, there were some very cute and truly unique Christmas decorations, but I must say they were very creative and suited the mountain setting perfectly. So I took the opportunity to take a few photos to keep as souvenirs. ![generated_image_169c](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWaYVyu85f53aSt5V6CEPwwMeWvzHe9WV9KHYxYH67EPd/generated_image_169c3d29-fc4e-49b6-afdf-a4726ef7ab00.png) Molveno has been receiving certifications for its tourist excellence for about 10 years. Also because in these parts I must say that they really take great care of the natural part and try to preserve it over time. Here we are not very high in altitude but the temperatures are quite cold especially in winter. That's why there are also many bars where you can take refuge and sip something hot. ![608338477_3927785060](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWauDHyRXo7yiXfh7aCj14FDmo78kLzY3TECBtDTsf2x6/608338477_3927785060698842_74319.jpg) Maybe I'll go back this summer, when the temperatures will be more pleasant, also because I would like to do some excursions not only on foot but also by mountain bike and around here there are several very interesting and very beautiful routes to do. To give you some tourist information, to get here you have to take the A22 motorway, exiting more or less at Trento Nord or San Michele and then there are 25 km of normal road to do and you reach the location very quickly, while as regards public transport, there are actually buses from Trento to Mezzocorona. But of course the journey is much slower and much more uncomfortable. Unfortunately, this location is not reached by train even if Trentino is famous for its private line that reaches most of these locations. I hope my photos were interesting and you liked them. See you soon. GPS: https://maps.app.goo.gl/PL5ih4pQtzwEV3M77 [//]:# (!steematlas 46.1428517 lat 10.96528244 long d3scr)

author: faisalamin
title: A Delicious Night Out After a Long Time
text:
Good Day Everyone!

Today, we enjoyed a special dinner after a long time from our favorite restaurant. We ordered beef karahi with hot, fresh chapati. The food was served in just 30 minutes, and we were very excited because it had been a while since we last visited. The taste was as delicious as always. We really enjoyed every bite of the meal. ![1000458017.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcqsZeatTRPKfNj5HHqCELU4VFHdskCnfBh3D1tCEEnNf/1000458017.jpg) After finishing the dinner, we treated ourselves to their special ice cream. It was a perfect end to the meal. I took some pictures of the food and the restaurant, and I am sharing them with you. It was a wonderful evening full of good food and happiness. The restaurant’s food never disappoints, and tonight was no different. ![1000458020.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZjwXEXSkPgqhDa5Bh7dHHSca8kCf6rZo88psuMA9fStR/1000458020.jpg) ![1000458019.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmawRrPkFXid8DoPEfeMtS9Pty9Y8Lf5dEYARpD1pjkoZB/1000458019.jpg) ![1000458018.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbmZuXYc7QURYKkSgSNdLiUMAdDA1R7gEGmnHWGV53Uyj/1000458018.jpg) We feel happy and satisfied after this special dinner. It was a lovely experience to go out and enjoy our favorite dishes again. We look forward to visiting the restaurant soon again.
###
